danger_signals.visual_form: Named antagonists (Snake, Gargamel) appear as stage hazards
danger_signals.meaning: Life-threatening obstacle requiring combat or evasion
Environmental friction and traversal difficulty indicate danger zones

danger_signals.visual_form: Shadow overlay rendering on specific character stages
danger_signals.meaning: Visual distinction of heightened difficulty or threat in particular Smurf's domain

safety_signals.visual_form: 'Plat' ground type in side-scrolling platform stages
Named bonus stages (Cicogne/Stork, Riviere/River) with 'vide' (empty) ground type
safety_signals.meaning: Optional safe collection areas outside main threat progression

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Character-specific Smurf variants (named types: Brainy, Luge, generic Smurf)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Smurf type system suggests visual differentiation between controllable Smurfs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage assignment by Smurf type indicates territorial/thematic domain control

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Named boss entities represent opposing force
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different boss types (Gargamel vs Snake) have distinct visual presence and combat mechanics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Boss encounters occur at stage conclusions, marking territorial incursion points

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard platformer level layout with ground type 'plat', sprite rendering with correct palettes
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player-controlled Smurf navigates terrain, encounters enemies, progresses toward boss

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ground type changes to 'glissant plat' (slippery flat), indicating physics state change
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement mechanics altered; reduced traction requires adapted traversal strategy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ground type 'mur' (wall), Smurf type display shows 'Luge', shadow layer removed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vertical or wall-based navigation required; distinct physics from standard platforming

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Shadow overlay layer rendered on environment, creating visual darkening effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased visual difficulty feedback; possible heightened enemy aggression or puzzle complexity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ground type 'vide' (empty), no Smurf type name displayed, isolated stage environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Collection-focused gameplay without standard combat progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Named antagonist sprite appears (Gargamel or Snake), stage environment isolates combat zone
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat mode engaged; boss entity becomes primary threat; standard navigation halted

environmental_storytelling.example: Stage ground types ('plat', 'glissant plat', 'mur', 'vide') communicate physical properties and gameplay mode without explicit HUD labels; French terminology suggests European origin and cultural localization depth
Specific Smurf types tied to specific stages (Brainy to shadow levels, Luge to snowsled stage) creates implicit narrative of character specialization and environmental domain ownership

environmental_storytelling.technique: World map structure with camera limitation
environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Map design deliberately hides full scope via camera panning, requiring navigation discovery rather than overview
environmental_storytelling.example: Southeast corner unrevealed during normal play; world-state obscured to enforce exploration narrative
Cutscenes and narrative transitions (accessible via debug mode Language Test) mark story progression and faction conflict escalation between boss encounters

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Requires multiple hits to defeat; can damage player on contact; represents primary antagonist threat
Requires multiple hits to defeat; can damage player on contact; distinct from Gargamel threat pattern
Navigates flat terrain, avoids ground hazards, progresses toward stage exit
Encounters shadow-rendered environment; likely puzzle or specialized challenge mechanics
Wall-based or vertical traversal; physics altered from standard platforming (wall/mur ground type)

notes: Source material provides debug interface documentation but minimal in-game visual communication details. Manifestation analysis derived from ground type system, stage variant naming, and character-stage assignment logic. No explicit color palette, faction symbols, or HUD documentation available. The game's visual communication relies on implicit environmental state coding (ground types) rather than explicit danger indicators. World map design deliberately obscures full state via camera mechanics, enforcing discovery-based narrative progression.
sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - The Smurfs SNES)
